Q12 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is a key drawback of Rejection Sampling when the observed evidence e is rare (i.e., P(e) is very small)?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
When P(e) is small, the sampler rejects almost all samples, making the algorithm extremely inefficient
and requiring an astronomical number of samples to converge.

Q16 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

When a MIN node evaluates a value returned by one of its children, which of the following is the correct action taken on the β value at that MIN node?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The MIN player always seeks to minimize the outcome, so β (the upper bound) is updated to the
minimum of its current value and the newly returned child value.

Q18 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct about search algorithms:

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
• IDS performs depth-limited DFS repeatedly with increasing limits.
It uses memory similar to DFS (very low space).
It is also complete like BFS because it eventually reaches the shallowest goal. So, option (a) is
correct.
• UCS does not use the heuristic h(n) for expansion.
It expands nodes in increasing order of path cost g(n).
Therefore, the statement is incorrect. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• A heuristic h(n) = 0 h(n) = 0 h(n) = 0 never overestimates the actual remaining cost.
This automatically satisfies the admissibility condition.
Thus, it is always admissible. So, option (c) is correct.
• Greedy Best-First Search uses only h(n), ignoring path cost.
Even with an admissible heuristic, it may choose a non-optimal route.
So it is not guaranteed to return the optimal path. So, option (d) is correct.

Q20 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Given input sequence p, q, r, t, s respectively. Which of the following permutations can be obtained in the output (in same order) using stack?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
• For option (b), push p, q then each of r, t, s respectively and pop them i.e. push(r), pop( ) then
perform push (t), pop( ), then push(s), pop( ), now perform two pop( ).
• Similarly operation (c) and (d) satisfied.

Q26 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In Likelihood Weighting, the weight of a sample wj is computed as the product of the conditional probabilities of the evidence variables E. If the evidence is E = {e1 – e1} and the remaining variables are Z, what is the expression for the sample weight wj?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The weight is the product of the conditional probabilities of all evidence variables, conditioned only on their parents, which must be either Z or E.

Q30 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following relational schemes:
R(A, B, C, D, E, F) and S(A, B, C) within the following functional dependencies:
{AB → C, C → ABDE, ADE → F}. Assume {A, B} is the key for both schemes.
Which of the following statements is true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
R is in 2NF and S is in 3NF and R covers S.
Because for the relation R FD’s are {AB → C, C → ABDE, ADE → F} out of these 3 FD’s 2nd one
is giving (non-key → non-key) so it is not in 3NF, but there are no partial dependencies, you
might be confused in 3rd FD (but it not partial FD).
Regarding relation S, it is in BCNF means it is also in 3NF right.
So option (b) is more appropriate.

Q31 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following defines the distance between two clusters Ci and Cj using the Complete Linkage clustering method, where D(x, y) is the dissimilarity measure between two objects?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Complete Linkage (maximum method) defines the distance between two clusters by the maximum
dissimilarity between any pair of objects, one from each cluster.

Q32 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A retail data warehouse stores sales information using a snowflake schema.
The Product dimension is normalized into Product, Category, and Department tables.
Which of the following is the MOST appropriate reason for choosing this schema?

Correct answer: B, A, C, D
Solution

(b)
Snowflake schema normalizes dimension tables into multiple related tables (e.g., Product →
Category → Department). The primary purpose of normalization in dimensions is to reduce
redundancy and storage for large, hierarchical dimension attributes.
Query performance may degrade due to additional joins, so (a) and (c) are incorrect. Option (d)
is unrelated.

Q35 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A researcher fails to reject the null hypothesis H0. Later, it is discovered that H0 was actually false. Which error has been committed?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Failing to reject H0 when it is actually false means the test missed a real effect.
This is exactly the definition of a Type-II error (β) accepting a false null hypothesis.

Q47 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements correctly describe the computational complexity of Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering (AHC) with N data points and D features? Select all that apply.

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
• Optimized algorithms, particularly for Single and Complete Linkage, can achieve improved
complexities, often Ο(N2 log N) or Ο(N2). So, option (b) is correct.
• A naive implementation involves V stages, and in each stage, it recalculates the Ο(N2) distances,
leading to Ο(N3) complexity. So, option (c) is correct.

Q54 NAT Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following relational instances:

 
Consider the following SQL queries Q1 and Q2.
Q1 : SELECT DISTINCT E.Ename, E.age FROM Emp_info
Q2 : SELECT E.Ename, E.age FROM Emp_info
Let X and Y be the number of tuples in the output of queries Q1 and Q2 respectively. Then (X2 – X1) is equal to ________.
NAT
Correct answer: 1
Solution

1
• Only 1 row in Emp_info is repeated twice.
• Distinct keyword will remove duplicates. Since there is only one duplicate, therefore (X2 – X1) will be equal to 1.
Hence (1) is the answer.

Q55 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

If the algorithm for reversing a singly linked list takes Θ(N) time and Θ(1) space, the algorithm is considered time-optimal and space-optimal for this problem because:

Correct answer: D, A
Solution

(d)
The problem inherently requires at least N operations (to visit and modify N pointers) and at
least constant auxiliary space, making the Θ(N) and Θ(1) solution theoretically optimal.

Q60 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Dijkstra’s algorithm, when implemented using an adjacency list with a min-priority queue, runs in Ο((V + E) log V) time.
S2 : Dijkstra’s algorithm without a priority queue (using a simple array) runs in Ο(V2) time.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• When Dijkstra’s algorithm is implemented using an adjacency list + min-priority queue (binary
heap), the time complexity becomes Ο((V + E) log V). So, S1 is correct.
• When Dijkstra’s algorithm is implemented using a simple array (no priority queue) to find the
minimum, each extract-min takes Ο(V), making the full algorithm Ο(V2). So, S2 is correct.

Q63 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : A stack cannot be implemented using a linked list.
S2 : In a linked list implementation of a stack, insertion and deletion are generally performed at the head of the list.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: B, A
Solution

(b)
• A stack can be easily implemented using a linked list by inserting and deleting from one end. So S1 is incorrect
• Insertion and deletion happen at the head in a linked-list-based stack.
This is true, because push/pop at the head gives Ο(1) performance without traversal. So, S2 is correct:
